---
title: "Problem Sets"
---

Two graded problem sets, done in groups. One submission per group, due on Canvas at **1:00 pm** — the start of the first section — regardless of which section you attend.

## Problem Set 1: Building the Cap Table {#ps1}

Follow Houzz from formation through a priced Series B: journal entries, cap tables round by round, an option-pool negotiation, and a recommendation to the founders.

| | |
|:--|:--|
| **Out** | Wednesday, September 9 (Session 2) |
| **Due** | Wednesday, September 23 at 1:00 pm |
| **Groups** | 2–4 students; one submission per group |
| **Points** | 40 — Part I 18 · Part II 10 · Part III 8 · Part IV 4 |

### How to do it

Work in the **[PS1 digital workbench](ps1/index.html)** — the full assignment is there: the case narrative, the pre-built cap-table grids and journal-entry widgets, and the submission generator.

1. Open the workbench and enter your group (2–4 names + NetIDs).
2. Work through Parts I–IV. Progress **autosaves to this browser only** — use *Export progress* / *Import progress* to hand off within your group.
3. When every part is complete, *Generate submission sheet* downloads one HTML file — **upload that file to Canvas** by the deadline.

The workbench handles structure and arithmetic hygiene — ownership percentages, balance warnings — and does **not** check answers. Every number entered is your group's own work; AI may be used as a study aid, but you must be able to reproduce and defend every figure.

### Downloads

- [Assignment (PDF)](files/ps1_assignment.pdf) — the same case and questions in print form, with exhibits; use it to read offline or as the traditional submission path if you can't use the workbench
- [Excel scratch template (optional)](files/ps1_student_template.xlsx) — blank tabs mirroring the four parts, for those who prefer to rough out the math in a spreadsheet first

---

## Problem Set 2: AI-Assisted Forecasting {#ps2}

Build a driver-based FY2027 operating forecast for Redline Labs, an AI contract-review startup, with AI tools doing the analyst work — and you certifying every number against the model and the verification log. Part A is a unit-economics warm-up (churn, retention, NRR, and spotting the AI's errors); Part B is the Excel build; Part C is scenarios, sensitivity, and a one-page board memo.

| | |
|:--|:--|
| **Out** | Monday, September 28 (Session 7) |
| **Due** | Monday, October 12 at 1:00 pm |
| **Groups** | Same groups as PS1; one submission per group |
| **Points** | 40 — Part A 8 · Part B 18 · Part C 10 · Verification Log 4 |

### How to do it

You hand in **three files**: your Excel model, a one-page board memo, and your workbench submission sheet.

1. Build the model in **Excel**, starting from `starter.xlsx` in the Model Pack below. Part B — the model itself — stays in Excel; clean model hygiene *in Excel* is the point.
2. Complete Part A, the five checksum certifications, the scenario and sensitivity readouts, and the Verification Log in the **[PS2 digital workbench](ps2/index.html)**. Progress **autosaves to this browser only** — use *Export progress* / *Import progress* to hand off within your group. When it is complete, *Generate submission sheet* downloads one HTML file.
3. Write the memo one-pager from `one_pager_template.pptx` and export it to a **one-page PDF**.
4. Put every group member's name on all three files and **upload all three to Canvas** by the deadline.

The workbench formats and flags (percentages, identity warnings, completeness); it does **not** check answers. **AI is required on this problem set**, and every number in your workbook and memo must be **human-certified**: you must be able to reproduce and defend each figure without the AI. Any number in your memo that cannot be traced to a cell in your model loses **2 points, each**.

::: {.callout-tip title="The AI workflow, in order (prompts P1–P6 on the prompt card)"}
1. **Extract** — P1, *Analyst*. Paste `case_facts.txt`; take back the driver table and driver tree. Anything not in the case text goes under OPEN QUESTIONS, not into the model.
2. **Benchmark** — P2, *Skeptic*. Classify each assumption aggressive / typical / conservative. Every external number traces to the benchmark sheet or a checkable source, or it gets stamped **UNVERIFIED** and never becomes a driver.
3. **Draft schedules** — P3, *Builder*. Take formula logic in plain words and build every cell yourself — the AI never touches the .xlsx. Hand-recompute one month, then confirm beginning + adds − churn = ending across all 12.
4. **Scenarios** — P4. Vary only the four named drivers. The AI proposes values; your own Excel data table decides which driver moves December cash the most.
5. **Red-team** — P5, *Board Red-Team*. Paste your one-pager numbers; take the five hardest board questions; recompute December top to bottom.
6. **Narrate** — P6, *Drafter*. The memo is written from verified numbers only — a bracketed placeholder beats an invented figure (that is the −2 rule).

Log as you go: every AI claim you rely on gets a Verification Log row **the moment you check it**, not reconstructed at the end. The full rules are in `ground_rules.pdf`; the exact prompts are on the prompt card.
:::

### Downloads

- [Assignment (PDF)](files/ps2_assignment.pdf) — the full case, questions, and Exhibit A in print form; the workbench mirrors it
- [Case facts (PDF)](files/ps2/case_facts.pdf) — the Redline Labs business and every stated assumption, with a one-table quick reference; your source of truth. Also as [plain text](files/ps2/case_facts.txt) for pasting into your AI tools
- [Driver data (CSV)](files/ps2/drivers.csv) — the numeric drivers that feed the model
- [Starter workbook (Excel)](files/ps2/starter.xlsx) — the tab skeleton you build on; treat it as an unverified v0 and audit every row
- [Benchmark sheet (PDF)](files/ps2/benchmark_sheet.pdf) — the peer benchmarks you may cite, with their provenance
- [Verification log template (Excel)](files/ps2/verification_log.xlsx) — the offline fallback for the log you complete in the workbench
- [Prompt card (PDF)](files/ps2/prompt_card.pdf) — starter prompts for driving the AI through the build
- [Ground rules (PDF)](files/ps2/ground_rules.pdf) — the AI-for-FP&A rules, including the benchmark-provenance rule
- [One-pager template (PowerPoint)](files/ps2/one_pager_template.pptx) — the board-memo layout you export to PDF
- [Fallback transcripts (ZIP)](files/ps2/fallback_transcripts.zip) — worked AI transcripts to fall back on if AI access fails mid-build

---

**Solutions and grading guides are on Canvas only** — they are never posted on this site.
